title: T-121 — packages/rate-limit middleware (Next.js + Server Actions, token-bucket, Upstash/in-memory) (P0)
prompt:
# T-121 — packages/rate-limit middleware

PRD: docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md §5.x Auth & Identity ("PIN entry is rate-limited identically to login (see lockout policy)"), §13 Niefunkcjonalne (uptime ≥99.5%)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create `packages/rate-limit` (`@monopilot/rate-limit`) exporting a Next.js middleware/Server-Action wrapper using a **token-bucket** algorithm backed by Upstash Redis in production and an in-memory store in tests. Pre-bind the SAML / SCIM / auth endpoints with sane defaults. Today auth, SAML, SCIM, and magic-link endpoints are unbound (auditor B §4.13 "Two module-local hits; no foundation primitive").

## Implementation contract
1. Create `packages/rate-limit/` workspace package. Deps: `@upstash/ratelimit`, `@upstash/redis`, `zod`. Dev: vitest.
2. `packages/rate-limit/src/store.ts` exports `Store` interface (`incr(key, windowMs): Promise<{ remaining, resetAt }>`) and two implementations:
   - `UpstashStore` (constructed from `UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL` + `UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN` env),
   - `InMemoryStore` (test-only).
3. `packages/rate-limit/src/limiter.ts` exports `createRateLimiter({ store, keyFn, limits: { capacity, refillPerSec }, name }): RateLimiter`. The token-bucket implementation uses `@upstash/ratelimit`'s `tokenBucket` algorithm in prod; in tests uses an algorithm-equivalent local impl. `RateLimiter.check(req): Promise<{ allowed: boolean; remaining: number; resetAt: number }>`.
4. `packages/rate-limit/src/next-middleware.ts` exports `withRateLimit(handler, opts): NextRequestHandler` for Route Handlers and `withRateLimitAction(action, opts): ServerActionHandler` for Server Actions. On deny: returns `429 Too Many Requests` with `Retry-After` header (route handler) or throws `RateLimitError` (server action).
5. `packages/rate-limit/src/presets.ts` exports pre-bound limiters for `auth-login`, `auth-magic-link`, `saml-login`, `scim-create`, `pin-verify` — limits seeded to: login=5/min/ip, magic-link=3/15min/email, saml-login=20/min/ip, scim-create=60/min/org, pin-verify=5/min/user.
6. Wire the presets into `apps/web/middleware.ts` (modify) — add a routing table that picks the right preset by URL pattern: `/api/auth/login` → auth-login, `/api/auth/magic-link` → auth-magic-link, `/api/auth/saml/*` → saml-login, `/api/scim/*` → scim-create. Other routes unaffected.
7. `packages/rate-limit/src/__tests__/limiter.test.ts` (integration):
   - With InMemoryStore: simulate 6 hits on a `5/min` limiter; assert first 5 allowed, 6th denied with `resetAt > now()`.
   - Assert key-function correctness (IP-based keys for login).
   - Assert `withRateLimit(handler)` returns 429 with Retry-After when denied.
   - Assert `withRateLimitAction(action)` throws `RateLimitError` when denied.
8. `apps/web/__tests__/middleware-rate-limit.test.ts`: hit `/api/auth/login` 6 times in test; assert 6th returns 429.

## Files in scope
- packages/rate-limit/package.json [create]
- packages/rate-limit/tsconfig.json [create]
- packages/rate-limit/eslint.config.mjs [create]
- packages/rate-limit/src/index.ts [create]
- packages/rate-limit/src/store.ts [create]
- packages/rate-limit/src/limiter.ts [create]
- packages/rate-limit/src/next-middleware.ts [create]
- packages/rate-limit/src/presets.ts [create]
- packages/rate-limit/src/__tests__/limiter.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/middleware.ts [modify — bind presets]
- apps/web/__tests__/middleware-rate-limit.test.ts [create]
- .env.example [modify — add UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL, UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given InMemoryStore-backed limiter configured 5/min/IP, when 6 requests arrive from same IP <60s, then requests 1-5 return {allowed:true} and request 6 returns {allowed:false, resetAt:future}.
2. Given withRateLimit(handler) wrapper, when denied request hits, then route handler responds 429 with Retry-After:<seconds> header (numeric).
3. Given withRateLimitAction(action) wrapper, when denied invocation occurs, then action throws RateLimitError carrying resetAt and name.
4. Given apps/web/middleware.ts updated, when 6 POST requests arrive at /api/auth/login in test, then 6th returns 429; AND Given UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL unset in test, when createRateLimiter called without explicit store, then uses InMemoryStore and logs one warning via T-117 logger; in production missing env throws fail-closed.
## Test strategy
- RED: write `limiter.test.ts` + `middleware-rate-limit.test.ts` for all 5 ACs before implementation. RED: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rate-limit test && pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run __tests__/middleware-rate-limit.test.ts`.
- GREEN: implement stores + limiter + Next middleware + presets + middleware.ts wiring.
- Run: above + `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rate-limit typecheck`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not key by `user_id` for unauthenticated routes — IP-based; once authenticated, you may shift to user-id key.
- Do not silently fall back to no-op limiting in production if Upstash is unreachable — fail-closed (return 503 or surface 429 to be safe); log the outage via T-117 + T-118.
- Do not store auth tokens or PINs in the rate-limit key — only IP / email-hash / user-id.
- Do not expose the `Retry-After` value when it leaks reservation count for security-sensitive routes (PIN verify) — use coarse `60` for those.
- Do not bypass rate-limit for `org.platform.admin` impersonation — the limit still applies (per audit log impersonation row).
- Do not skip the `apps/web/middleware.ts` change — the limiter is useless if not bound.

pipeline_inputs.description: @monopilot/rate-limit package with Upstash/in-memory token-bucket store + Next middleware/Server-Action wrappers + presets for auth-login/magic-link/saml/scim/pin-verify. Bound in apps/web/middleware.ts. Vitest integration on fake handler verifies allow/deny.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Token-bucket via @upstash/ratelimit in prod, in-memory in tests. Pre-bound presets: login=5/min/ip, magic-link=3/15min/email, saml=20/min/ip, scim=60/min/org, pin-verify=5/min/user. withRateLimit wraps Route Handlers (429 + Retry-After); withRateLimitAction wraps Server Actions (throws RateLimitError). middleware.ts routes by URL prefix. Production fail-closed on Upstash outage.

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
acceptance_criteria: Given InMemoryStore-backed limiter configured 5/min/IP, when 6 requests arrive from same IP <60s, then requests 1-5 return {allowed:true} and request 6 returns {allowed:false, resetAt:future}.
Given withRateLimit(handler) wrapper, when denied request hits, then route handler responds 429 with Retry-After:<seconds> header (numeric).
Given withRateLimitAction(action) wrapper, when denied invocation occurs, then action throws RateLimitError carrying resetAt and name.

Given apps/web/middleware.ts updated, when 6 POST requests arrive at /api/auth/login in test, then 6th returns 429; AND Given UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL unset in test, when createRateLimiter called without explicit store, then uses InMemoryStore and logs one warning via T-117 logger; in production missing env throws fail-closed.

GREEN: implement stores + limiter + Next middleware + presets + middleware.ts wiring.
risk_red_lines: Do not key by user_id for unauthenticated routes — IP-based; after auth, may shift to user-id.
Do not silently fall back to no-op limiting in production on Upstash outage — fail-closed (429 or 503).
Do not store auth tokens or PINs in rate-limit keys — IP / email-hash / user-id only.
Do not expose Retry-After leaking reservation count on PIN-verify — use coarse 60 for security-sensitive routes.
Do not bypass rate-limit for impersonation — same limit applies.
Do not skip apps/web/middleware.ts change — limiter is useless if unbound.
